item.title: Intro to Data Compression with OCaml: Prefix-Free Codes
item.description:
Introduction

There’s no better time to learn about data compression than now, where “big data” is now just “data.” But even before large scale data analysis was commonplace, limited bandwidth and storage capacity encouraged the design of good compression algorithms. The enduring relevance and importance of compression has convinced me to dig in and learn the basics of data compression, as well as relevant information theoretic concepts.

item.title: A Primer on Provenance in Databases
item.description:
Introduction

Although research on data provenance extends back decades, increasing commodification and sharing of data has placed new salience on provenance. Knowing, inter alia, the origin, chain of ownership, and what transformations the data has undergone is useful across fields. For example, having a complete provenance can

  • prevent (or catch) the misappropriation of proprietary or private data,
  • help data users better understand their data,
  • audit data sharing practices between organizations.

Given provenance’s growing applicability, this blog post provides a short introduction to provenance in relational databases. To be sure, capturing the provenance of data stored in other formats is important. See, e.g., The C2PA’s standard for provenance for images. But relational databases continue to be one of the most commonly used models and, by all indications, are here to stay.

item.title: My First Post with Sidenotes
item.description:
Welcome to My Blog

This is a sample post demonstrating sidenotes This is a sidenote! It appears in the margin and provides additional context without interrupting the main text flow. in Hugo using the xmin theme.

Sidenotes are a great way to add You can add formatted content in sidenotes, including emphasis and links. supplementary information to your content without cluttering the main narrative.

How to Use Sidenotes

Simply use the shortcode in your markdown:

item.title: Will the Real Gen Z Please Stand Up
item.description:
Will the Real Gen Z Please Stand Up

In the age of big data, generation groups seem almost quaint. Despite the daily classification of individuals into ever finer classes by tech companies, Male, 27, afraid of heights. we continue to generalize about the tendencies of people born more than a decade apart. As somone on the borderline of two groups, associating with either feels absurd. Too young to remember 9/11, too old for ChatGPT in school, just in time for Diary of a Wimpy Kid (the book). How much do I really have in common with a 13-year old?

item.title: Supra Sucks
item.description:
Supra Sucks

Of all the Bluebook’s rules, supra is among the worst. Pity the reader? No, make them work. You want the reference? Find it yourself. Seeing a supra halfway through an article is like drawing a Go Back 20 Spaces card in a board game.

It’d be one thing if people read articles top to bottom. At least then you’d have some chance of knowing what a footnote reading “See Smith supra n.28” referred to. But who does that? No one. God help you if the article cites multiple works from the same author.
